Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

I tested the patch on Debian 9 amd64 and it works:
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor      4083 Feb  5 13:46 lustre_2.12.0-dirty-1_amd64.changes
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor      1756 Feb  5 13:36 lustre_2.12.0-dirty-1.dsc
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor  15309160 Feb  5 13:36 lustre_2.12.0-dirty-1.tar.gz
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor  15701408 Feb  5 13:56 lustre-client-modules-4.9.0-8-amd64_2.12.0-dirty-1_amd64.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    488476 Feb  5 13:46 lustre-client-utils_2.12.0-dirty-1_amd64.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    428388 Feb  5 13:46 lustre-dev_2.12.0-dirty-1_amd64.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    129158 Feb  5 13:46 lustre-iokit_2.12.0-dirty-1_amd64.deb

-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor   8115966 Feb  5 13:46 lustre-tests_2.12.0-dirty-1_amd64.deb

Compiling on armv6l (qemu) raspbian latest is still compiling and building, and looks good.
I guess it will take at least several hours to finish, if an error occurs I comment.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

I managed to get it compiling and building on RPI2 and raspbian,
and the header files for rpi are missing in control and control.main,
that is: linux-headers-rpi2 needs to be added.

Patch Set 3: -Code-Review

Still getting a lot of compiling errors on RPI2 (raspbian)

/home/tstibor/dev/lustre/lustre-release-lu-11880/lustre/ptlrpc/../../lustre/ldlm/ldlm_pool.c:722:16: error: initialization from incompatible pointer type [-Werror=incompatible-pointer-types]
 LUSTRE_RW_ATTR(recalc_period);
                ^
include/linux/sysfs.h:104:11: note: in definition of macro ‘__ATTR’
  .store = _store,      \
           ^~~~~~
/home/tstibor/dev/lustre/lustre-release-lu-11880/lustre/include/lprocfs_status.h:814:30: note: in expansion of macro ‘LUSTRE_ATTR’
 #define LUSTRE_RW_ATTR(name) LUSTRE_ATTR(name, 0644, name##_show, name##_store)
                              ^~~~~~~~~~~
/home/tstibor/dev/lustre/lustre-release-lu-11880/lustre/ptlrpc/../../lustre/ldlm/ldlm_pool.c:722:1: note: in expansion of macro ‘LUSTRE_RW_ATTR’

 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/home/tstibor/dev/lustre/lustre-release-lu-11880/lustre/ptlrpc/../../lustre/ldlm/ldlm_pool.c:722:16: note: (near initialization for ‘lustre_attr_recalc_period.store’)
 LUSTRE_RW_ATTR(recalc_period);
                ^
include/linux/sysfs.h:104:11: note: in definition of macro ‘__ATTR’
  .store = _store,      \
           ^~~~~~
/home/tstibor/dev/lustre/lustre-release-lu-11880/lustre/include/lprocfs_status.h:814:30: note: in expansion of macro ‘LUSTRE_ATTR’
 #define LUSTRE_RW_ATTR(name) LUSTRE_ATTR(name, 0644, name##_show, name##_store)
                              ^~~~~~~~~~~
/home/tstibor/dev/lustre/lustre-release-lu-11880/lustre/ptlrpc/../../lustre/ldlm/ldlm_pool.c:722:1: note: in expansion of macro ‘LUSTRE_RW_ATTR’
 LUSTRE_RW_ATTR(recalc_period);
 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/home/tstibor/dev/lustre/lustre-release-lu-11880/lustre/ptlrpc/../../lustre/ldlm/ldlm_pool.c:729:16: error: initialization from incompatible pointer type [-Werror=incompatible-pointer-types]
 LUSTRE_RW_ATTR(limit);
Topic set to ARM

Patch Set 3:

There are a number of other patches that are needed to build on arm6l (AFAIK RPi2) and arm7l (RPi3), which I've tagged with the "ARM" topic:

 https://review.whamcloud.com/#/q/topic:ARM

This patch is just about identifying that Raspbian is a dpkg-based distro.  Rather than building an exhaustive list of such distros, instead just key on the fact that "dpkg" is installed.

Patch Set 3:

> near initialization for ‘lustre_attr_recalc_period.store’)
>  LUSTRE_RW_ATTR(recalc_period

This is patch https://review.whamcloud.com/33920 ("LU-8066 ldlm: match store macro prototype") which is already landed to master.  I have 4 other patches that are also needed to get this working reasonably.

Patch Set 3:

Ok, thanks, I will try to put the patches on top.
On my raspbian none of the header file packages (linux-headers-generic | linux-headers | linux-headers-amd64) from control file exists,
so I guess only the proper RPI header file package is needed.

Patch Set 3:

> On my raspbian none of the header file packages (linux-headers-generic
 > | linux-headers | linux-headers-amd64) from control file exists,
 > so I guess only the proper RPI header file package is needed.

Just to clarify, do you want the debian/control file updated to conditionally require the linux-headers-rpi2 package for Raspbian builds before giving this +1?  That is beyond my understanding of Debian packaging.  The main goal of this patch was to fix the obvious errors in the control file that prevented me from building the Lustre .dpkg after the appropriate files were installed. 

I'd prefer changes to the build requirements be done in a separate patch, because in my case I ended up installing "raspberrypi-kernel-headers" that matched the installed "raspberrypi-kernel" package, so I don't have the linux-headers-rpi2 package installed:

 $ lsb_release -a
 Distributor ID:	Raspbian
 Description:		Raspbian GNU/Linux 9.6 (stretch)
 Release:		9.6
 Codename:		stretch

Patch Set 3:

Andreas, sorry for the confusion. I cherry-picked all the required/tagged ARM patches:

>git log -5 | grep LU
    LU-11880 build: allow Debian build on Raspbian
    LU-11770 osc: allow build without blk_integrity or crc-t10pi
    Fixes: ccf3674c9ca ("LU-10472 osd-ldiskfs: T10PI between RPC and BIO")
    LU-10171 lmv: avoid gratuitous 64-bit modulus
    Fixes: 9b924e86b27d ("LU-10171 headers: define pct(a,b) once")
    LU-11878 tests: don't fork-bomb sanity test_103b
    Fixes: 543f1fbe260 ("LU-10830 utils: fix create mode for lfs")
    LU-11830 lov: avoid signed vs. unsigned comparison

and it built the DEB packages as intented (except modules) on my RPI system:

Distributor ID:	Raspbian
Description:	Raspbian GNU/Linux 9.6 (stretch)
Release:	9.6
Codename:	stretch
Linux indigopi 4.14.79-v7+ #1159 SMP Sun Nov 4 17:50:20 GMT 2018 armv7l GNU/Linux

-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    456704 Feb  6 16:31 lustre-client-utils_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_armhf.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    694214 Feb  6 16:31 lustre-client-utils-dbgsym_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_armhf.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    407090 Feb  6 16:31 lustre-dev_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_armhf.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    129192 Feb  6 16:31 lustre-iokit_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_armhf.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor 122823916 Feb  6 16:30 lustre-source_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_all.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor   8113678 Feb  6 16:33 lustre-tests_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_armhf.deb
-rw-r--r-- 1 tstibor tstibor    451936 Feb  6 16:32 lustre-tests-dbgsym_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_armhf.deb

The building process stopped at:

 dpkg-genbuildinfo
 dpkg-genchanges  >../lustre_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1_armhf.changes
dpkg-genchanges: error: file ../lustre_2.12.51-6-gccfb565-1.tar.gz has checksum 8083fa1475f8e56bbf4383666b85415d instead of expected 15fcec4eec560d6f3ca79bab77ebc415 (algorithm md5)
dpkg-buildpackage: error: dpkg-genchanges gave error exit status 255
autoMakefile:1208: recipe for target 'debs' failed
make: *** [debs] Error 255

It stopped with an incorrect checksum error. I will check why. Does "make debs" run through on your RPi?

commit.subject: LU-11880 build: allow Debian build on Raspbian
commit.message:
LU-11880 build: allow Debian build on Raspbian

Allow the Debian build to work on Raspbian and other Debian and
Ubuntu variants that use the same build machinery.  Add armhf
to the list of allowed CPU architectures.

Test-Parameters: trivial clientdistro=ubuntu1804
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Iedc90ad32754f81a3d387a66409f07aaa305a5b1
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/34085
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Thomas Stibor <t.stibor@gsi.de>
Reviewed-by: Minh Diep <mdiep@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
